metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4066
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In C. elegans, secreted proteins are endocytosed and degraded by specialized scavenger cells termed coelomocytes. Once taken up by coelomocytes, fluorescent proteins accumulate within endolysosomal organelles, which can be visualized as large internal fluorescent patches. Proneuropeptide genes NLP-21-yellow fluorescent protein (YFP) or INS-22-YFP were expressed in cholinergic motor neurons (using the unc-129 promoter) and were detected in coelomocytes (Figure 7a). In order to ensure that the fluorescent patches are indicative of neuropeptides, soluble YFP or GFP-SNB-1 was expressed in motor neurons (using the same promoter) and it failed to produce detectable coelomocyte fluorescence (Figure 7b).

Figure 7 C and D: Coelomocyte fluorescence decreased by 40% in PKC-1 mutants. This defect was completely rescued by restoring the expression of PKC-1 in cholinergic neurons using the unc-17 promoter. Unc-31 mutants were used as a positive control in the experiment.
These results suggest that PKC-1 acts in motor neurons promote neuropeptide secretion.

In order to verify that PKC1 effects DCV release and not the production or transport of neuropeptides, distribution of NLP-21 and INS-22 fluorescence puncta was quantified in the ventral cord of PKC-1 mutants.
Figure 8 B and C: NLP-21 and INS-22 puncta fluorescence were both significantly increased (~25%) in pkc1 mutants and the effect was rescued by transgenes expressing PKC-1 in cholinergic neurons. NLP-21-YFP and INS-22-YFP puncta fluorescence increased similarly in unc-31 CAPS and unc-13 Munc13 mutants. Unc-31 CAPS and unc-13 Munc13 mutants are known to play a vital role in DCV exocytosis.

Concluding from Figure 7 and Figure 8, PKC1 is responsible for regulating presynaptic dense-core vesicle exocytosis. The term 'regulating' was used on the basis of the kinase activity of PKC1 protein.. Experimental description: Transgenic strains were generated by injecting pkc-1(nj3) and the expression construct with the coinjection markers KP#708 (Pttx-3::rfp), KP#1338 (Pttx-3::gfp) or pPD118.33 (Pmyo-2::gfp) using standard methods (PMID: 1935914)

pkc-1 constructs that rescued pkc-1(nj3) mutants were: KP#1374, KP#1375, KP#1376, KP#1377. KP#1374 contains pkc-1B cDNA and a 3,214-bp unc-17 promoter fragment. KP#1375 contains pkc-1A cDNA and the unc-17 promoter fragment. KP#1376 contains pkc-1B cDNA and a 3,011-bp snb-1 promoter fragment. KP#1377 contains pkc-1B with an ~4-kb pkc-1 promoter fragment.
